close_reason:
Epic complete: Replaced gobuckify with buckgen

Created new buckgen tool that parses Go files directly (no go list scaffolding needed).

Components built:
- src/go/pkg/goparse/ - Go file parser with build constraint support
- src/go/pkg/buckgen/ - BUCK file generation library
- src/cmd/buckgen/ - CLI tool

Issues resolved:
1. Fixed double vendor/ path in deps
2. Used directory name for target naming (consistency)
3. Added package_name attribute for correct import resolution
4. Filtered self-references from deps
5. Filtered test files when collecting imports
6. Fixed srcs glob to include assembly (.s, .S) files
7. Excluded stub Go files (function declarations only) from srcs

Note: Users may need to run `rm -rf .devenv .turnkey/godeps && direnv reload` after pulling these changes to force a fresh godeps cell rebuild.
description:
Currently gobuckify uses `go/packages` to analyze code, which requires valid Go modules that can be imported. This means go-deps-cell.nix has to generate dummy `main.go` and `go.mod` files just to make gobuckify happy.

A cleaner approach would parse `.go` files directly to extract:
- Package declarations
- Import statements  
- Build constraints (OS/arch-specific files)

This would eliminate the need for the godeps cell to contain scaffolding code.

May require forking gobuckify to implement this differently.
title: Revisit gobuckify to not require importable code
